heading: Posthumous awards
label: 8300 Posthumous awards
text: If a person who distinguishes himself dies before an award to which he is entitled is made, the award may be made and the medal, cross, or bar, or associated emblem or insignia may be presented, within five years from the date of the act or service justifying the award, to his representative as designated by the President.

Prior ProvisionsA prior section 8300, acts , ; , , ; , , ; , , , provided for selection board procedure when promotion-list officers in regular grade of first lieutenant, captain, or major are to be considered for promotion under , prior to repeal by , , , effective .
